content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

May 14, 1997


Timothy J. Tucker
Director of Candidate Services
Los Angeles City Ethics Commission
201 N. Los Angeles St.
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Re:     Your Request for Advice
        Our File No. A-97-176

        This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the filing officer provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

The City Ethics Commission is the filing officer for campaign disclosure statements filed
by candidates for elective city office, their controlled committees, and committees primarily
formed to support or oppose city candidates and ballot measures. As such, you are required to
determine whether campaign statements conform on their face with the reporting requirements of
the Act. You have asked the following questions with regard to the requirement to report the
occupation and employer of contributors of $100 or more:

1.      When a contributor's employer information is left blank or contains a description
        such as "self," must the filing officer request the committee to amend the
        statement and provide the business name pursuant to section 84211(d)(4)?

2.      If it is the case that a self-employed contributor's business has no name, must the
        filing officer request a committee to list the contributor's name in the employer
        column on the form?




¹Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18100-18995
of the California Code of Regulations.


        428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814   (916) 322-5660

Timothy J. Tucker
Our File No. A-97-176
Page 2

CONCLUSION

1. When a filing officer discovers during his or her review of campaign statements
that a candidate or committee has filed an incorrect, incomplete, or illegible
statement, an amendment must be requested. However, amendments need not be
requested when errors or omissions are minor or do not recur throughout the
campaign statement and do not result in omission of the amount of an individual
contribution or expenditure. An error or omission in connection with
identification of a contributor is minor if the contributor is identified by name and
either street address, occupation, employer, or principal place of business.

2. When a self-employed contributor's business has no name, it is sufficient if the
filer uses the word "same" or some other indication that there is no business
name.

ANALYSIS

Section 84211 sets out detailed campaign reporting requirements for candidates and
committees as defined in the Act. For contributions received, candidates and committees must
provide:

"If the cumulative amount of contributions (including
loans) received from a person is one hundred dollars ($100) or
more and a contribution has been received from that person during
the period covered by the campaign statement, all of the following:
(1) His or her full name.
(2) His or her street address.
(3) His or her occupation.
(4) The name of his or her employer, or if self-employed,
the name of the business.
(5) The date and amount received for each contribution
received during the period covered by the campaign statement and
if the contribution is a loan, the interest rate for the loan.
(6) The cumulative amount of contributions."

Section 84211(f).
(Emphasis added.)

Original campaign statements of local candidates and committees are filed with city and
county filing officials, who are required to determine whether the documents conform on their
face with the requirements of the Act and Commission regulations. (Sections 84215, 81010.)
Regulation 18110 (copy enclosed), which specifies the duties of filing officers with regard to

Timothy J. Tucker
Our File No. A-97-176
Page 3

campaign reports, states that the filing officer must review all statements to ensure that all of the
required information is contained in the statement, including:

"For each person listed as a contributor or lender of a
cumulative amount of $100 or more, the complete name, address,
occupation and employer, if any (or name of business if described
as self-employed), cumulative amount contributed, date and
amount of contribution, and if the contribution is a loan, the
interest rate for the loan. 84211(f)."

Regulation 18110(b)(2)(E)(8).

However, regulation 18110 goes on to state:

"In those cases where the filing officer discovers in his or
her review of campaign statements that a candidate or committee
has filed an incorrect, incomplete or illegible campaign statement
or a campaign statement which cannot be reproduced, he or she
shall promptly notify the candidate or committee of the error or
omission. However, no notification is required in those cases in
which the errors or omissions are minor ones which do not recur
throughout the campaign statement. An error or omission is minor
if it does not result in omission of the amount of an individual
contribution or expenditure. An error or omission in connection
with the identification of a donor or intermediary is minor if such
person is identified by name and either street address, occupation,
employer or principal place of business. An error or omission in
connection with the identification of the recipient of an expenditure
or person providing consideration for an expenditure is minor if
such person is identified by name."

Regulation 18110(c)(3).
(Emphasis added.)

Nothing in the Act or regulations prohibits a filing officer from requesting amended
statements when information is missing from a candidate or committee's statement. However, as
stated above, the filing officer is not required to request an amendment for minor errors or those
which do not recur throughout the campaign statement.

In the Marrs Advice Letter, No. A-78-200 (copy enclosed), we advised that it was
sufficient to use "same" or a similar notation in the "Employer" column when a self-employed
contributor did not have a separate business address, e.g., a self-employed farmer. Although

Timothy J. Tucker
Our File No. A-97-176
Page 4

business addresses are no longer required to be disclosed on campaign statements, it would be
acceptable to use the same method of disclosure where a self-employed contributor has no
business name which is different from the name of the contributor or the name of the contributor
is wholly contained within the name of the business, e.g., Mary Smith, M.D., Inc.

If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[handwritten signature: Carla Wardlow]

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosures

Timothy J. Tucker
